NOTICE OF AND MOTION FOR ORDER DEEMING ADMISSIONS ADMITTED (TRANSACTION ID # 100039436) FILED BY PLAINTIFF ABSOLUTE RESOLUTIONS CORPORATION HEARING SET FOR DEC-21-2018 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, PLAINTIFF STIPULATES TO THE MATTER BEING HEARD BY THE PRO TEM JUDGE. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: NO OPPOSITION HAVING BEEN FILED IN RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF ABSOLUTE RESOLUTIONS CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER DEEMING ADMISSIONS THE COURT ORDERS AS FOLLOWS: 1. DEFENDANT PAULA LONG HAFDAHL WILL SERVE ON PLAINTIFF FULL AND COMPLETE CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSION WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, 2. IF DEFENDANT FAILS TO COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF PARAGRAPH (1) ABOVE, ALL OF THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WILL BE DEEMED TO HAVE BEEN ADMITTED AND DEFENDANT WILL BE PRECLUDED FROM CHALLENGING THE TRUTH OF THOSE ADMISSION. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WERE SOUGHT AND NONE WILL BE IMPOSED. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS INSTRUCTED TO PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT IT TO DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM, AND DELIVER A COPY TO THE COURT FOR REVIEW AND SIGNATURE. JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL RENNE; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED (302/JPT)

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar May-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ABSOLUTE RESOLUTIONS CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. No opposition filed. Judgment to be entered in favor of plaintiff. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare and submit a proposed order that repeats the Court ruling verbatim and proposed judgment.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-25-2019 Continued To Jun-03-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-28-2019 Continued To Mar-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-28-2019 Assigned To Trial Court

Notice Of And Motion For Order Deeming Admissions Admitted
Off Calendar Dec-21-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. STEVE LEVY, ESQ. of Law Offices of Steve Levy (Tel: 800-637-6922) via CourtCall for plaintiff Absolute Resolutions Corporation;
No other appearances made.

Plaintiff stipulates to the matter being heard by the pro tem judge. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

No opposition having been filed in response to plaintiff absolute resolutions corporation's motion for an order deeming admissions the court orders as follows:
1. Defendant Paula Long Hafdahl will serve on plaintiff full and complete code compliant responses to plaintiff's request for admission within ten (10) days from the date of this order,
2. If defendant fails to comply with the requirements of paragraph (1) above, all of the requests for admission will be deemed to have been admitted and defendant will be precluded from challenging the truth of those admission.
No monetary sanctions were sought and none will be imposed.

Plaintiff's counsel is instructed to prepare an appropriate Order, submit it to defendant for approval as to form, and deliver a copy to the court for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-07-2018 Set For Trial On Jan-28-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
